questions.question: What is a mineral? Explain its characteristics.
questions.answer: A mineral is a homogeneous, naturally occurring substance with a definable internal structure. Minerals are found in rocks and have specific physical and chemical properties such as hardness, colour, density and crystal structure.

questions.question: Explain why minerals are considered indispensable for human life.
questions.answer: Minerals are indispensable because almost everything we use is made from minerals. They are used in buildings, machinery, vehicles, electricity production and even food contains minerals required for body functions.
answer_outline: Role of minerals in daily life

questions.question: Explain the different modes of occurrence of minerals in nature.
questions.answer: Minerals occur in igneous and metamorphic rocks in veins and lodes, in sedimentary rocks as beds or layers, as residual deposits like bauxite, as alluvial deposits called placers, and dissolved in ocean water.

questions.question: Explain how minerals occur in igneous and metamorphic rocks.
questions.answer: In igneous and metamorphic rocks minerals occur in cracks, faults or joints. They are formed when molten or gaseous minerals rise through cavities and solidify as they cool.

questions.question: Explain the formation of minerals in sedimentary rocks.
questions.answer: In sedimentary rocks minerals are deposited in layers through processes of accumulation and concentration. Coal and iron ore are examples formed due to heat and pressure over long periods.

questions.question: Describe placer deposits and give examples.
questions.answer: Placer deposits are minerals found in sands of valley floors and river beds. These minerals resist corrosion and include gold, silver, tin and platinum.

questions.question: Explain the distribution of iron ore in India.
questions.answer: Iron ore is mainly found in Odisha-Jharkhand belt, Durg-Bastar-Chandrapur belt, Ballari-Chitradurga belt in Karnataka and Maharashtra-Goa belt.

questions.question: Explain the importance and uses of manganese.
questions.answer: Manganese is mainly used in manufacturing steel and ferro-manganese alloys. It is also used in bleaching powder, insecticides and paints.

questions.question: Explain the importance of copper and its major producing areas in India.
questions.answer: Copper is malleable, ductile and a good conductor. It is used in electrical cables and electronics. Major producers include Balaghat in Madhya Pradesh, Khetri in Rajasthan and Singhbhum in Jharkhand.

questions.question: Explain the formation and distribution of bauxite in India.
questions.answer: Bauxite is formed by decomposition of rocks rich in aluminium silicates. Major deposits are found in Amarkantak plateau, Maikal hills and Koraput district of Odisha.

questions.question: Explain the properties and uses of mica.
questions.answer: Mica splits easily into thin sheets and has excellent insulating properties. It is widely used in electrical and electronic industries.

questions.question: Explain the importance of limestone.
questions.answer: Limestone is a sedimentary rock composed mainly of calcium carbonate. It is the basic raw material for the cement industry and used in iron smelting.

questions.question: Discuss the hazards of mining.
questions.answer: Mining causes health hazards like lung diseases among miners and environmental problems such as water pollution, land degradation and air pollution.

questions.question: Why is conservation of minerals necessary?
questions.answer: Minerals are finite and non-renewable resources formed over millions of years. Excessive extraction leads to depletion and rising costs.

questions.question: Explain different conventional sources of energy.
questions.answer: Conventional sources include firewood, coal, petroleum, natural gas and electricity. These sources have been traditionally used for energy production.

questions.question: Explain the formation and types of coal.
questions.answer: Coal is formed from plant material buried under heat and pressure. Types include peat, lignite, bituminous and anthracite.

questions.question: Explain the distribution of coal in India.
questions.answer: Coal is mainly found in Gondwana coalfields like Jharia, Raniganj and Bokaro and also in tertiary coalfields in Meghalaya, Assam and Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: Explain the importance of petroleum and its distribution in India.
questions.answer: Petroleum provides fuel, lubricants and raw materials for industries. Major producing areas include Mumbai High, Gujarat and Assam.

questions.question: Explain the importance of natural gas.
questions.answer: Natural gas is used as a fuel for electricity generation, heating in industries, fertilizer production and as cooking fuel.

questions.question: Explain how electricity is generated.
questions.answer: Electricity is generated by hydroelectric power using flowing water and by thermal power using coal, petroleum or natural gas.

questions.question: Explain the concept of nuclear energy.
questions.answer: Nuclear energy is produced by altering the structure of atoms. Uranium and thorium are used to generate atomic power.

questions.question: Explain the importance of solar energy in India.
questions.answer: India receives abundant sunlight and solar energy can be converted into electricity using photovoltaic technology.

questions.question: Explain the use of wind energy in India.
questions.answer: Wind energy is used to generate electricity through windmills. Tamil Nadu, Gujarat and Maharashtra have major wind farms.

questions.question: Explain the working and benefits of biogas plants.
questions.answer: Biogas is produced from decomposition of organic waste like cattle dung. It provides energy and produces high-quality manure.

questions.question: Explain the importance of conserving energy resources.
questions.answer: Energy conservation is necessary for sustainable development. Using renewable energy, public transport and energy-efficient devices helps reduce energy consumption.
